_patch_meta.produced_by: Claude AI — Registry Mode Phase C (second pass)
_patch_meta.description: Dimension assignments for 5 new shalom subform groups: 2509-001/002 (H7965I well-being), 2511-001 (H7965K greeting), 2510-001 (H7965J friendship), 406-001 (H7965H Peace [God]). Registry stamp included.
_patch_meta.normalization_note: CC-side label normalisation 2026-05-02: canonical DIMENSION_LABEL prefix + spacing applied ("Vitality/Existence" -> "07 — Vitality / Existence", etc.). Researcher waiver extends to this second-pass H7965 DimReview cycle. Source patch retained at v1; this v2 is the applied form.

set.notes: Shalom as the comprehensive condition of welfare and wholeness of the whole person — health, safety, soul-safety, and prosperity as divinely given or sustained. Psa 38:3 (no health in bones because of sin) locates welfare-absence in the body caused by inner disorder; Psa 55:18 (soul redeemed in safety) locates the welfare-condition in the soul; Jer 29:11 (plans for welfare and hope) names divine purposing for comprehensive flourishing. Vitality/Existence preferred over Emotion-Positive (scope is broader than felt-state) and Relational Disposition/GOD (the condition belongs to the human person even when divinely given). Dominant subject HUMAN: the welfare condition is borne by the human even where God is its creator and sustainer.

operations.description: 2509-001: shalom as inner condition of welfare — Vitality/Existence, HUMAN

set.notes: Shalom as the falsely claimed welfare arising from stubbornness of heart — the morally-generated false inner assurance of safety. Deu 29:19 (blesses himself in his heart, walking in stubbornness) is the defining anchor: the self-deception is seated in the heart and produced by the moral disposition of stubborn covenant-unfaithfulness. Jer 23:17 confirms the pattern (false welfare promised to those who follow their own stubborn hearts). Moral Character (05) preferred: the false sha.lom is the expression of a stable inner quality of moral nature (stubbornness/unfaithfulness) rather than primarily an emotion (02) or cognitive error (03). Dominant subject HUMAN throughout.

operations.description: 2509-002: shalom as falsely promised welfare — Moral Character, HUMAN

set.notes: Shalom as the substantive blessing-content invoked in greeting — the inner orientation of goodwill toward another expressed as the peace-blessing (1Sa 25:6: Peace be to you, and peace be to your house, and peace be to all that you have). The greeting-blessing is fundamentally a relational act of goodwill; the sha.lom invoked is the expression of the greeter's orientation toward the recipient's comprehensive welfare. Relational Disposition (06) preferred over Emotion-Positive (which would name the condition wished for rather than the act of orientation). Single-verse evidence: VERSE_EVIDENCE_MINIMAL noted; call is unambiguous. Dominant subject HUMAN.

operations.description: 2511-001: shalom as peace-blessing in greeting — Relational Disposition, HUMAN

set.notes: Shalom as the inner relational bond of close friendship — the orientation of trust, loyalty, and allegiance toward a specific other person. 1Ch 12:17 (my heart will be joined to you if you come in sha.lom) directly ties sha.lom-friendship to heart-union: the sha.lom is the inner orientation whose seat is the heart. The betrayal pattern across the corpus (Psa 41:9, 55:20, Jer 20:10, 38:22) confirms that the sha.lom-bond was a real inner orientation — its violation wounds precisely because it was genuine. Relational Disposition (06): the inner orientation of loyalty toward another, stronger than Moral Character (05) here because the primary characteristic is the relational bond (directed toward another) rather than a stable quality of moral nature considered in itself. Dominant subject HUMAN: human-to-human friendship bond throughout.

operations.description: 2510-001: shalom as inner bond of friendship — Relational Disposition, HUMAN

set.notes: Shalom as divine identity commemorated in the altar name YHWH-Shalom (Judg 6:24) — God named as peace itself in the human act of altar-building following the peace-theophany (Judg 6:23: Peace be to you. Do not fear). The characteristic operates at the divine-human boundary: God discloses peace (divine pole), Gideon receives it and names God as its substance (human pole). DHC (11) assigned: this parallels the structure of 2508-004 (Mic 5:5 — He shall be their peace, DHC, NONE) but here the divine pole dominates — the content named is God's identity as peace, the naming act is human but secondary. Dominant subject GOD: YHWH-Shalom names God's identity; the human act (Gideon builds and names) is the vehicle, not the subject. Analytical note: the 'X IS peace' structure appears at three points in Scripture — YHWH (Judg 6:24, this group), the Messiah (Mic 5:5, group 2508-004), and Christ (Eph 2:14, R117 Greek group 426-003) — forming the structural spine of the peace registry. Subsumed in DIM-117-SD003.

operations.description: 406-001: shalom as divine identity YHWH-Shalom — Divine-Human Correspondence, GOD
Registry stamp: R117 peace dim_review_status = Complete (second pass — all 78 groups at CLAUDE_AI)
